title: vaccinium shastense subsp. nevadense
intro: Vaccinium shastense subsp. nevadense, also known as the Nevada blueberry, is a plant species that belongs to the heath family and is native to the western United States. It is used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infections. The health benefits of Vaccinium shastense subsp. nevadense include its ability to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared for use in herbal medicine through various methods, including making teas, tinctures, and infusions from its leaves and berries.
